paper_title: The PMIP4 contribution to CMIP6 – Part 2: Two interglacials, scientific objective and experimental design for Holocene and Last Interglacial simulations

methods.description: This method describes the process of evaluating the realism and performance of climate model simulations (specifically PMIP4-CMIP6 simulations for the mid-Holocene and Last Interglacial periods) by comparing their outputs to various paleoenvironmental observations and climate reconstructions. The comparison involves both qualitative and quantitative approaches, considers uncertainties in the paleo data, and utilizes different data types and compilation strategies depending on the time period and available data.

steps.description: Obtain climate model simulation outputs for the target interglacial period (mid-Holocene or Last Interglacial).
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch climate model simulation outputs for past interglacial periods.
inputs: Climate model simulation results (e.g., PMIP4-CMIP6 outputs)
outputs: Climate model simulation data (e.g., temperature, precipitation, vegetation, water balance)

steps.description: Obtain relevant paleoenvironmental data and climate reconstructions for the target period. This includes data from various archives (e.g., lake levels, pollen, marine cores, ice cores, tree rings, speleothems, corals, molluscs) and types (qualitative observations, quantitative climate variable reconstructions, records of climate variability).
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch paleoenvironmental data and climate reconstructions for past interglacial periods.
inputs: Target time period (mid-Holocene or Last Interglacial)

steps.description: Perform qualitative comparisons between simulated outputs and paleo observations, such as comparing simulated water balance against lake-level reconstructions or simulated vegetation patterns against pollen-based vegetation reconstructions.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare simulated climate patterns qualitatively with paleo reconstructions.
inputs: Climate model simulation data (e.g., water balance, vegetation patterns)
Qualitative paleo environmental data (e.g., lake levels, vegetation patterns)

steps.description: Perform quantitative comparisons between simulated climate variables and quantitative climate reconstructions (e.g., comparing simulated temperature or precipitation values with reconstructed values).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare simulated climate variables quantitatively with paleo reconstructions.
inputs: Climate model simulation data (e.g., temperature, precipitation)
Quantitative paleo climate reconstructions (e.g., temperature, precipitation values)
outputs: Metrics of model performance (e.g., biases, spatial correlations)

steps.description: (Optional/Advanced) Use process-based models to translate climate model outputs into explicit simulations of specific paleo-records (e.g., simulating pollen assemblages or isotopic signals based on climate outputs) for a more direct comparison with the raw paleo data.
steps.searchable_summary: Simulate paleo-records using process-based models driven by climate model outputs.
outputs: Simulated paleo-records (e.g., simulated pollen data, simulated isotopic data)

steps.description: Account for uncertainties associated with paleo-reconstructions (including statistical uncertainties and those resulting from incomplete process understanding) when performing data-model comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: Incorporate paleo-reconstruction uncertainties into data-model comparisons.
outputs: Robust model evaluation considering data limitations

steps.description: (Specific for Last Interglacial) Utilize LIG data compilations that employ harmonized chronologies and are presented as time slabs (e.g., 2000-year intervals) to mitigate issues related to dating uncertainties and non-synchronous climate changes when comparing with time-slice simulations (like lig127k).
steps.searchable_summary: Use LIG paleo data compiled with harmonized chronologies and time slabs for comparison.
inputs: Last Interglacial climate model outputs (e.g., lig127k simulation)
Last Interglacial paleo data compilations with harmonized chronologies and time slabs
outputs: LIG comparison results based on specific time slabs

steps.description: (Specific for Holocene Transient Simulations) Assess the plausibility of transient simulations by comparing the simulated temporal evolution of climate with available paleo time series data.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare temporal evolution of climate simulations with paleo time series.
inputs: Holocene transient climate model outputs (time series)
outputs: Evaluation of simulated temporal trends and variability

steps.description: Utilize multiple independent reconstruction data sets for a comprehensive diagnosis of the model simulations, identifying areas of agreement and disagreement across different data sources.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform data-model comparisons using multiple independent paleo data sets.
outputs: Identification of consistent/inconsistent findings across data sets
